title: Data & Knowledge Systems / Epistemic Humility (IS 331)
frameworks: Relational Model (Codd 1970): data as mathematical relations, SQL as declarative query
ACID: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability — the four guarantees of reliable transactions
CAP Theorem (Brewer): Consistency, Availability, Partition tolerance — pick two
Knowledge Graph: entities + relationships + properties modeled as a mathematical graph
Vector Embeddings + Cosine Similarity: convert meaning to geometry, measure understanding as distance
Hybrid Retrieval: graph context + vector similarity for comprehensive knowledge access
concepts: Vocabulary as database index: named concepts are indexed lookups, vague language is full-table scans
ACID properties cost performance — understanding the tradeoff is engineering judgment
Normalization eliminates redundancy; denormalization optimizes read performance — context determines which
SQL injection is the canonical lesson in never trusting user input
Graph theory (Euler, 1736) is the mathematical foundation of network analysis, social networks, and knowledge graphs
Vector embeddings convert meaning into geometry — cosine similarity measures conceptual distance
Semantic search finds meaning, not just keyword matches
Epistemic Humility: knowing what you don't know, and having a plan to find out
context: IS 331 is Studio 5 of the BSEAI degree. Students build comprehensive data systems spanning relational databases, graph databases, and vector stores. The core capability is Epistemic Humility — knowing the limits of what data can tell you and what it hides. Assessment: 20% quizzes / 30% participation / 50% final project.
